description: Redesign plugin and tool status indicators: remove all bottom-right dots (green/yellow/update), replace with border-based activity flash and opacity-based readiness, move update notification to the PluginMenu dropdown badge.

userStories.title: Replace PluginIcon StatusIndicator dot with border-based readiness and activity
userStories.description: As a user, I want plugin icons to communicate readiness and activity through their border treatment instead of small overlay dots, so the status is more visually integrated and easier to read at a glance.
acceptanceCriteria: The StatusIndicator sub-component inside PluginIcon is removed entirely — no green dot, no yellow dot, no ArrowUp update badge
Ready plugins: icon container has `border-2 border-border` (solid black/gray border in light/dark mode), active SVG is shown
Not-ready plugins (tabState 'unavailable' or 'closed'): icon container has `border-2 border-border/30` (faded ghost border), inactive SVG is shown (existing SVG swap logic preserved)

When a plugin has an active tool invocation: the icon container border animates with a new `animate-activity-border-flash` animation that pulses the border color to `border-primary` (yellow) using the same 0.4s ease-in-out infinite cycle as the existing activity-flash keyframe
When a tool invocation ends (active transitions false→true→false): the border color fades back from primary to border-border over 500ms using a new `animate-activity-border-fade-out` animation
The `hasUpdate` prop is removed from PluginIcon and StatusIndicator — update indication is no longer handled by the icon
The `active` prop on PluginIcon still works but now drives the border animation instead of a dot
PluginIcon.stories.tsx is updated: remove ReadyWithUpdate story, update all stories to reflect the new border-based states, add stories showing border flash (active) and border fade-out

userStories.title: Replace ToolIcon activity dot with border-based activity flash
userStories.description: As a user, I want individual tool icons to show activity through their border pulsing yellow instead of a small green dot, so the visual language is consistent between plugin-level and tool-level activity indicators.
acceptanceCriteria: The green activity dot element (8px circle at bottom-right) is removed from ToolIcon entirely
When a tool is actively being invoked (active=true): the tool icon container border pulses yellow using the same `animate-activity-border-flash` animation added in US-001
When activity ends: the border fades back via `animate-activity-border-fade-out` over 500ms
Enabled + idle: `border-2 border-border bg-primary` (unchanged yellow bg, solid border)
ToolIcon.stories.tsx is updated to reflect the new border-based activity states
userStories.notes:
Files to edit:
- `platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/ToolIcon.tsx` — Remove the green dot element (lines 51-60). The fade-out logic (useRef/useEffect on lines 19-31) stays but now drives border classes instead of dot visibility. The container div (line 34-39) changes:
  - Enabled + idle: `border-2 border-border bg-primary` (same as now)
  - Enabled + active: `border-2 bg-primary animate-activity-border-flash` (border pulses yellow via animation)
  - Enabled + fading out: `border-2 bg-primary animate-activity-border-fade-out`
  - Disabled + idle: `border-2 border-border/40 bg-muted/40` (same as now)
  - Disabled tools cannot be active so no disabled+active state needed

  The existing `cn()` call on lines 35-38 already conditionally applies classes based on `enabled`. Add `active` and `fadingOut` conditions to the same `cn()` call.

- `platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/ToolIcon.stories.tsx` — Update the `Active` and `ActiveDisabled` stories. The visual result is now a border flash instead of a dot. Consider adding an `ActiveFadeOut` story that demonstrates the fade-out transition.

IMPORTANT: The tool icon bg is already `bg-primary` (yellow) when enabled. The border flash also uses yellow (`border-primary`). This is intentional — the border pulses to match the background, creating a 'glow' effect where the border and background merge during the flash peak. At the trough (50% of the cycle), the border returns to `border-border` (black), creating a visible pulse. This reads clearly because the border is a different surface from the fill.

userStories.title: Add update notification badge to PluginMenu trigger button
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see an update notification badge on the plugin's dropdown menu button when an update is available, so I know to click the menu and update the plugin.
acceptanceCriteria: When `plugin.update` exists, the PluginMenu trigger button (kebab icon) shows a small yellow dot (4-5px) at the top-right corner of the button
The dot uses `bg-primary` (yellow) with `border-2 border-background` for contrast
When no update is available, no dot is shown
The 'Update to vX.Y.Z' menu item inside the dropdown still works as before
PluginMenu.stories.tsx is updated with stories showing the update badge state
The update tooltip on the PluginIcon in PluginCard.tsx is updated to remove the update reference since the icon no longer shows update state (the tooltip can still show version + trust tier)
userStories.notes:
Files to edit:
- `platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/PluginMenu.tsx` — The trigger button is on lines 39-44. It's a `<button>` with `h-6 w-6` containing `<MoreHorizontal>`. Wrap it in a `relative` container (or make the button itself `relative`) and add a conditional dot:
  ```tsx
  <button type="button" className="relative flex h-6 w-6 items-center justify-center rounded hover:bg-muted/50" aria-label="Plugin options">
    <MoreHorizontal className="h-4 w-4" />
    {plugin.update && (
      <div className="absolute top-0 right-0 h-1.5 w-1.5 rounded-full border border-background bg-primary" />
    )}
  </button>
  ```
  The dot is 6px (h-1.5 w-1.5) with a 1px border for contrast. Position at top-0 right-0. Use `rounded-full` (this is one of the few places circles are appropriate in the brutalist theme — notification badges are universally round).

- `platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/PluginCard.tsx` — The tooltip content on lines 159-162 currently shows version, trust tier, and update info. Remove the update line from the tooltip since the icon no longer communicates update state:
  ```tsx
  <Tooltip.Content>
    v{plugin.version} · {plugin.trustTier}
  </Tooltip.Content>
  ```
  The `hasUpdate={!!plugin.update}` prop was already removed from PluginIcon in US-001.

- `platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/PluginMenu.stories.tsx` — Add stories showing the menu trigger with and without the update badge. The `plugin` prop in stories needs an `update` field to show the badge.

userStories.title: Clean up unused CSS animations and verify E2E
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the old activity-flash/fade-out CSS animations cleaned up if they are no longer referenced, and the full verification suite to pass including E2E tests.
acceptanceCriteria: If the old `activity-flash` and `activity-fade-out` keyframes and classes in styles.css are no longer referenced by any component, they are removed
If they ARE still referenced (e.g., by other components not modified in this PRD), they are kept
All imports of removed types/props across the codebase are cleaned up (grep for `hasUpdate` in all side-panel component files to ensure no stale references)
userStories.notes:
This is a cleanup story. After US-001, US-002, and US-003, the old green/yellow dot animations may no longer be used.

- `platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/styles.css` — Search for references to `animate-activity-flash` and `animate-activity-fade-out` across the entire `platform/browser-extension/src/` directory. If no component references them, remove the keyframes and classes (lines 144-169 in the current file). The new `animate-activity-border-flash` and `animate-activity-border-fade-out` classes added in US-001 remain.

- Grep for `hasUpdate` across all files in `platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/` to ensure no stale prop references exist after US-001 removed it from PluginIcon.

- Grep for `bg-success` in side-panel components — the green dot used `bg-success`. If no component references it after the dot removal, that's expected (the token stays in the theme since Alert and other primitives may use it).

- Run the full suite including E2E to verify the visual changes don't break any browser automation tests.
